# LegalLean - Verified Legal Reasoning in Lean 4

> A framework for encoding laws as machine-checkable types, using Lean 4 dependent types. 44 machine-verified theorems across 3 jurisdictions. Open source (MIT).

## What LegalLean Is
LegalLean is a verification layer for legal AI. It encodes legal rules as Lean 4 propositions and proves their properties with mathematical certainty. The key innovation is FormalisationBoundary: a type that explicitly represents where machine verification ends and human judgment begins (Hart's "open texture").

## Key Facts
- 44 machine-verified theorems, 0 sorry stubs
- 3 case studies: US tax law (IRC §163(h)), immigration (O-1A visa), Australian telecoms (TCP Code)
- Docker-verified reproducibility in 15 seconds
- Open source under MIT licence

## Core Types
- FormalisationBoundary: formal | boundary | mixed
- LegalRule: defeasible rules with defeat relations
- Deontic: obligation | permission | prohibition
- EvidenceFor: typed evidence witnesses

## Hardening Methodology
Factorial/combinatorial testing of the NL-to-Lean translation layer. Canonical typed IR, Lean-as-oracle equivalence proofs (T1 ↔ T2), false precision detection, 3-way covering arrays.

## Links
- Project site: https://legal-lean.aguilar-pelaez.co.uk
- GitHub: https://github.com/edu-ap/legal-lean

- Commercial: https://legalengine.co.uk/legal-lean/
